FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class Reorderable

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

E5-L3 — drag-reorder for ANY one-axis item list (incl. Virtual.List), composed from the engine layers beneath it (plan Part B E5; user ruling: the Flutter Draggable/DragTarget + react-beautiful-dnd + SwiftUI model, deliberately NOT WinUI's OLE loop): each item is an L2 DragSource of Kind, the list body is ONE accepting DropTargetSpec, the slot math is ReorderList (WinUI's midpoint rule + live-reorder dwell — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:984-1063, :50-51), sibling displacement is the engine FLIP pipeline (each wrapped item carries a LayoutTransition; re-rendering the PROJECTED order moves them through their springs), the ghost/lift visuals and the drop-glide are L1 DragController, and edge auto-scroll is engine-level in DragDropContext (WinUI's 100px/150–1500px/s gradient) — this class adds none of those mechanics, only the list semantics.

Usage (inside a component render):

var ro = UseMemo(() => new Reorderable("track") { OnReorder = (f, t) => Move(tracks, f, t) });
ro.Scene = Context.Scene; ro.RequestRender = Context.RequestRerender;
ro.ItemCount = tracks.Count; ro.ItemExtent = 48f;
return ro.List(Virtual.List(tracks.Count, 48f,
    slot => ro.Item(ro.ItemAt(slot), RowFor(tracks[ro.ItemAt(slot)]), key: tracks[ro.ItemAt(slot)].Id)));

Render slots through ItemAt(int) with STABLE per-item keys: mid-drag the projection moves the dragged item to the dwell-committed slot, the keyed diff keeps node identity, and the FLIP pass animates the displaced siblings (the part-to-make-room motion) while DragController.Move re-anchors the pointer-held visual.

CROSS-LIST: two Reorderables sharing a Kind. The target list accepts the foreign session, shows the insertion line while hovered, and on drop routes the ONE OnCrossCommit (target's, else the source's) — the source's gesture completion then cancels its local reorder instead of committing (no double mutation).

KEYBOARD lift mode (the react-beautiful-dnd a11y pattern): Space on a focused item lifts it (a pointer-free reorder; the item dims to the L1 drag opacity with the lifted shadow), Up/Down (Left/Right when Horizontal) move the insertion slot with NO dwell, Space drops (commits), Escape cancels, and focus leaving the lifted item cancels. All slot moves re-render the projection → FLIP animates them.

public sealed class Reorderable

Constructors

Reorderable(string) 

public Reorderable(string kind)

Parameters

kind string

Fields

AutoDwell

Advance the dwell from the wall clock on every drag delta (default). Set false for deterministic tests and drive Advance(float) yourself (or set DwellMs = 0).

public bool AutoDwell

Field Value

bool

ExtentOf

Variable extents: resting main-axis extent per ORIGINAL index (sampled once at lift into pooled storage). Cross-list insertion math still assumes the uniform ItemExtent pitch.

public Func<int, float>? ExtentOf

Field Value

Func<int, float>

Horizontal

Horizontal strip (TotalDx drives the slot math; Left/Right are the keyboard-lift arrows).

public bool Horizontal

Field Value

bool

ItemCount

Item count this render (drives Begin(int, ReadOnlySpan<float>, float) at lift).

public int ItemCount

Field Value

int

ItemExtent

Uniform resting main-axis extent per item (ignored when ExtentOf is set).

public float ItemExtent

Field Value

float

ItemOf

The app item carried as Item (what a foreign target receives).

public Func<int, object?>? ItemOf

Field Value

Func<int, object>

LiftOpacity

Keyboard-lift visual opacity — the SAME lift visual the pointer path gets from L1 (WinUI ListViewItemDragThemeOpacity = 0.80 — microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\ListViewItem_themeresources.xaml:7, identical in all ThemeDictionaries).

public const float LiftOpacity = 0.8

Field Value

float

LiveProject

Live mid-drag projection (default): ItemAt(int) returns the PROJECTED order while lifted, so displaced siblings FLIP to make room (the rbd motion). REQUIRES key-preserving children (plain keyed children / ForEl / Repeater): the keyed diff moves nodes with their items. Set FALSE over Virtual.List/VirtualListEl — its window diff recycles nodes POSITIONALLY (no keys), so a mid-drag projection would swap content under the lifted node; instead the resting order holds during the drag and the insertion LINE shows the pending slot (commit still lands exactly where shown).

public bool LiveProject

Field Value

bool

OnCrossCommit

CROSS-LIST commit (see ReorderCrossCommit). The drop TARGET's delegate runs; when it has none, the SOURCE's is used — set the one shared callback on both lists.

public ReorderCrossCommit? OnCrossCommit

Field Value

ReorderCrossCommit

OnReorder

SAME-LIST commit: (fromIndex, toIndex) in the ORIGINAL order — remove at from, insert at to (Move<T>(IList<T>, int, int) applies it to an IList<T>).

public Action<int, int>? OnReorder

Field Value

Action<int, int>

RequestRender

Re-render request (wire Context.RequestRerender or a signal bump): fired whenever the projected order / lift / insertion-line state changed and the consumer must re-render the list.

public Action? RequestRender

Field Value

Action

Scene

The scene (wire Context.Scene) — needed only for CROSS-LIST insertion math (pointer → slot) and the insertion-line offset; same-list reorder works without it.

public SceneStore? Scene

Field Value

SceneStore

ShowInsertionLine

Show the built-in 2px accent insertion line while a FOREIGN session hovers this list, and for same-list feedback when LiveProject is off. Default true.

public bool ShowInsertionLine

Field Value

bool

Spacing

The container's main-axis Gap between items.

public float Spacing

Field Value

float

Properties

Core

The slot-math core (midpoint rule + dwell + displacement hints + projection). Exposed for advanced composition; normal consumers only read the surface properties here.

public ReorderList Core { get; }

Property Value

ReorderList

DwellMs

Live-reorder dwell before displaced items shift (WinUI LISTVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER 200ms / GRIDVIEW 300ms — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:50-51). 0 ⇒ displacement follows the pointer immediately.

public float DwellMs { get; set; }

Property Value

float

InsertionIndex

The slot the line marks: the foreign session's insertion slot (0..ItemCount), else the same-list pending slot (−1 when nothing shows).

public int InsertionIndex { get; }

Property Value

int

InsertionLineOffset

Main-axis offset (list-wrapper space, scroll-corrected) of the insertion boundary — where the built-in InsertionLine() sits. Foreign sessions mark the slot boundary; a same-list (LiveProject-off) lift marks the resting start the dragged item will land at (DraggedTargetStart... computed at the PENDING slot for immediacy).

public float InsertionLineOffset { get; }

Property Value

float

InsertionVisible

The insertion line is showing: a foreign session hovers the list, OR a same-list lift with LiveProject off has a pending slot away from home (the virtual-list feedback mode).

public bool InsertionVisible { get; }

Property Value

bool

IsKeyboardLifted

True while the lift is the pointer-free keyboard mode.

public bool IsKeyboardLifted { get; }

Property Value

bool

IsLifted

A lift is in flight on THIS list (pointer drag or keyboard lift).

public bool IsLifted { get; }

Property Value

bool

Kind

The drag kind this list emits AND accepts (the cast-free L2 discriminator).

public string Kind { get; }

Property Value

string

LiftedIndex

The lifted item's ORIGINAL index (−1 when idle).

public int LiftedIndex { get; }

Property Value

int

TargetIndex

The dwell-committed slot the projection currently shows (−1 when idle).

public int TargetIndex { get; }

Property Value

int

Methods

Advance(float)

Deterministic dwell driver (tests / a FrameClock subscriber when AutoDwell is off). Returns true when the shown target changed (a re-render was requested).

public bool Advance(float dtMs)

Parameters

dtMs float

Returns

bool

InsertionLine()

The built-in cross-list insertion indicator: a 2px accent bar at the insertion boundary, stretched across the list (rbd's insertion cue; WinUI's native cue is displacement, which same-list reorder gets via FLIP). Non-hit-testable. Consumers composing their own List(Element) wrapper can place it manually from InsertionVisible/InsertionLineOffset.

public Element InsertionLine()

Returns

Element

Item(int, Element, string?, LayoutTransition?)

Wrap one item's content: an L2 DragSource of Kind (payload = ReorderPayload, resolved once at promotion), the L1 lifecycle wired into the slot math, a FLIP LayoutTransition (default Slide) for the displacement motion, focusable with the keyboard lift-mode keys, and the keyboard-lift visuals when lifted that way (the pointer path gets identical visuals from L1). index is the ORIGINAL item index (pass ItemAt(slot)).

public Element Item(int index, Element content, string? key = null, LayoutTransition? transition = null)

Parameters

index int
content Element
key string
transition LayoutTransition?

Returns

Element

ItemAt(int)

The ORIGINAL item index shown at slot under the current projection (identity when idle): render slot s with the item at ItemAt(s) and the item's STABLE key — mid-drag the dragged item occupies the dwell-committed slot and the keyed diff + FLIP animate the displaced siblings. With LiveProject off this stays identity while lifted (virtual-list mode — the insertion line is the feedback; the commit reorders the model itself).

public int ItemAt(int slot)

Parameters

slot int

Returns

int

List(Element)

Wrap the list body as the ONE accepting drop target of Kind (a ZStack so the insertion-line overlay can ride above the body): same-list releases commit through the gesture completion, foreign sessions get the insertion line + OnCrossCommit. Captures the wrapper node for the pointer→slot math (set Scene).

public Element List(Element body)

Parameters

body Element

Returns

Element

OffsetFor(int)

Displacement hint passthrough (OffsetFor(int)) for offset-hint consumers that render the RESTING order with translation hints instead of the projected order. Do not combine an authored offset hint with a FLIP Animate on the same node (see the ReorderList class remarks).

public float OffsetFor(int originalIndex)

Parameters

originalIndex int

Returns

float
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
